reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures all elements of the informal statement. Points in ℝ² are represented as `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2)`, integer coordinates are expressed via `hint`, and distinctness via `htriangle`. The expression `dist A B + dist B C` matches |AB|+|BC| literally (B as the middle vertex). `(MeasureTheory.volume (convexHull ℝ {A,B,C})).toReal` equals the standard area of triangle ABC (Lebesgue measure of the filled triangle). The `threesquare P Q R` predicate asserts |QP|=|QR| and (P-Q) ⊥ (R-Q), which together with distinctness forms a right isoceles triangle at Q. The disjunction over (A,B,C), (B,C,A), (C,A,B) covers all three possible corners, and three points forming a right isoceles triangle is equivalent to being three vertices of a square. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
